column10: A vaccine is a special kind of medicine that helps keep people from getting sick. Usually a doctor or nurse will use a syringe with a small needle to give a person a vaccine shot in their arm or leg. Sometimes, it can also come as drops that you swallow. A vaccine, also called an immunization, helps your body learn what some bad germs called viruses or bacteria are like, so your body can practice and get better at fighting them. The first vaccine was made over 200 years ago by Edward Jenner in England to stop people from getting a disease called smallpox. Today, vaccines are used all over the world to help protect people from many different diseases and illnesses, and people are making new vaccines to protect against new diseases.

column10: A turtle is an animal with four legs, a small tail, a head, and a shell on its back. Turtles can be found nearly all over the world and can live in many places—some like to swim in water, while others live on land. Tortoises' feet are similar shapes to elephants' feet for walking on land, and turtles' feet are webbed for swimming. If a turtle feels scared, it can pull its head and legs inside its shell to hide. Fossils show that turtles have been living on Earth for more than 200 million years! Turtles come in many sizes, shapes, and colors. Tortoises and turtles eat plants, but many turtles also eat a variety of other creatures including insects and fish.

column10: A sock is a soft piece of clothing a person wears on their foot. A sock often looks like a stretchy cloth tube or bag with a closed end for the toes and foot and an open end that pulls up to the ankle or leg. Socks protect the skin on feet by helping keep feet dry and a comfortable temperature. People make socks out of many materials and in many sizes and designs. People in Egypt and Greece used pieces of cloth or animal skin around their feet to make socks more than a thousand years ago.

column10:
A Petoskey stone is a special rock that is found along the shores of the Great Lakes in Michigan. A Petoskey stone is really a fossil–the remains of an under-water coral animal that lived about 350 million years ago! Long ago, Michigan was covered by a warm, shallow sea, and these corals lived there. When the corals died, they sank to the bottom of the sea and over millions of years, they slowly turned into stone.
Looking at a Petoskey Stone, especially if it's wet or polished, you can see gray or brown six-sided shapes that fit together like a puzzle. These shapes were once the tiny little homes where the coral animals lived! Each circle with a dot in the middle was one tiny coral creature.

column10: Ballet shoes are light, soft, smooth shoes that dancers wear on their feet when they do a kind of dancing called ballet. Ballet shoes are usually made from leather, canvas, or satin which can be soft, smooth, and silky. The shoes fit snug so the dancer can feel the floor as they dance and move in careful, graceful ways. There are also special stiff ballet shoes called pointe shoes that let trained dancers balance on their tip-toes. People danced ballet more than 500 years ago in Italy. People have been making and using ballet shoes for about 300 years in France. Dancers all over the world use ballet shoes today.

column10:
A horse is a big animal with four strong legs, hooves, and long hair on its head, neck, and tail. Horses are mammals, like humans, who drink milk from their moms when they’re babies. A baby horse is called a foal and can stand and walk within the first hours it is born. Horses are strong runners with good balance who can bend down to sip water, eat grass(es), and even sleep while standing up. 

[Fossil scientists think the first members of the Equida (which includes Horses, Zebras, and Donkeys) lived 55 million years ago—after dinosaurs & before people.] The types of horses we know of today have been around for more than 4 million years and first lived in North America. Around 6,000 years ago people near Kazakhstan worked with horses--using them to carry heavy loads, help farmers, and travel. 

Before cars and trains, people used to ride horses to get around. Today, people across the world love to ride horses and have them as pets.

column10: A great white shark is a large fish that lives in the ocean. It's known for its powerful body and sharp teeth. These sharks can grow very big, some can be as long as a school bus! Great whites are part of the shark family and only live in water. These sharks are meat eaters (carnivores) and very protective of their home (territorial).
column12: We do not know exactly how long great white sharks have been around, but scientists have found fossils dating them as far back as the Miocene Epoch period. This was a period of time before any humans were alive.
column14: Great white sharks originated in the ocean in the Miocene Epoch period, they are evolved from shark species even bigger than them. The world was very different back then and the location of all the places in the world were different. That means great white sharks come from a part of the ocean that is not there anymore. Now they are found all over the ocean, anywhere in the ocean that has cool salt water. Some places you might find sharks today are the coasts of South Africa, California, Australia, and New Zealand.

column10:
A ski is a flat, narrow object that people can use to slide on top of snow. One goes on each foot. The person using one or two skis wears special boots that attach to the ski with something called a binding. The shape of a ski is about 4 inches wide, and they come in different lengths depending on the person's height. One end of the ski is rounded and curved upward to keep it gliding over bumpy surfaces of snow. The bottom of each ski has a slippery surface to allow it to slide over the snow.

People tend to use skis to travel across land, or down hills. Downhill skiing is something people do for fun, where they take a motorized lift to the top of the mountain, and they slide down wide paths that have been cleared of trees. Traveling across snow-covered land is often called cross-country skiing, which also has developed into a sport.

column10:
Sunglasses are special lenses people wear to protect their eyes and help them see when there is bright light. Sunglass lenses are made of tinted glass that filters or collects some of the light before it reaches your eyes--allowing some but not all light to pass through.

The Inuit people of Canada used snow goggles, an early version of sunglasses, more than one thousand years ago to protect their eyes from bright sunlight reflected off the snow and ice.

Today, different types of sunglasses are worn all over the world to protect eyes on bright sunny days, when looking directly at lights (like during a dentist's appointment), when looking at the sun during a solar eclipse, or while driving or flying.

column10:
A Game Boy Color is a small object you can hold in your hands that lets you play different older games. It was made by a company called Nintendo, and it first came out almost  30 years ago.
The Game Boy Color looks a bit like a small, thick rectangle, usually about the size of a small book but much thinner. It has a little screen near the top, a set of buttons on the front, and a space in the back where you place the game. 
What made it special was that its screen could show games in color for the first time, not just black, white, and gray. It let players enjoy great games like Pokémon, The Legend of Zelda, and Super Mario on the go, with bright, moving pictures in their hands.

column10:
Sushi is a food from the Japanese culture typically made by putting seafood on top of rice. The rice is made sticky by soaking it in vinegar. Sushi can be made with lots of different ingredients, like how there are lots of different things that can go on pizza. Common ingredients for sushi are: Salmon, Yellowtail, Seaweed, and Imitation Crab Meat.
In Countries like China and Japan, fish have been stored in rice soaked vinegar since medieval times. This was originally done to keep the fish from spoiling, but eventually Japanese people began eating the rice with the fish. By the 1800s, around when the first trains were invented, sushi as we know it today was invented by Hanaya Yohei.

column10:
Ice cream is a sweet and cold desert enjoyed around the world. It is made by freezing and stirring cream at the same time. This mixes small amounts of air into the cream as bubbles, giving Ice cream a smooth and soft texture. Ice cream can be almost any flavor because other ingredients can be mixed in with the cream while it's being stirred. Some flavors you may have seen are chocolate, vanilla, mint, and straberry.
Frozen treats have been around for most of human history, but Ice Cream as we know it today was invented in the 1600s. This was around the time when Shakespear wrote his plays and the Pilgrims came to the America. The first recorded recipe for Ice Cream was made by Antonio Latini, an italian chef who recorded the first Sorbet recipie in 1692. Before this, Indian cooks had made a similar ice cream called Kulfi.

A pen is a small tool you hold in your hand to make marks on paper. When you move it, it leaves a line, so you can draw pictures, write your name, or make little doodles. It usually has a skinny body, a pointy tip, and ink inside that comes out when you touch it to paper.
A very long time ago, people used reeds, which are plant stems, dipped in ink to write. We think some of the first pens like this were used in a place called ancient Egypt. Over many years, people made new kinds of pens that were easier to use, and that is how we got the pens we have today.

column10:
One day, Lewis Waterman was about to sign an important business contract.

But when he used his pen… it leaked!
Ink spilled all over the paper and ruined the deal. 

Because of that accident, he lost the customer.

So he thought:
“There must be a better pen that doesn’t leak.”

He worked hard and invented a new kind of fountain pen that controlled the ink flow.

And that’s how the modern fountain pen was born!

A mountain is a very tall natural landform that rises high above the land around it, making it a type of place on Earth. Mountains formed billions of years ago when huge pieces of the Earth slowly pushed together or when volcanoes built up layers of rock, and no person made them—they come from natural forces deep inside the planet. They are found all over the world and often have rocky sides, plants, or snowy tops, with the very top called a peak. Mountains are special because they are some of the highest places on Earth, can be very cold at the top, and help create rivers and weather, and even though they can look different—some sharp, some round—they are all known for being much taller than the land nearby.

column10: Hot chocolate is a warm, yummy drink made from chocolate melted into milk! It is sweet and cozy. This drink is perfect for chilly days. People have been making drinks from cocoa for a very long time. Long ago, people in Mesoamerica, more specifically Mexico, discovered that the seeds inside the cacao tree's fruit could be made into a special drink. Back then, it was more bitter and spicy, different than the sweet drink we are used to today! When explorers were introduced to the drink from Mexico, they brought it to Europe and people started adding sugar and warm milk! That's how we got the hot chocolate we know and love today.

In Native American folklore, especially for the Ojibwe people in North America, the Loon is very important. According to their creation stories, the loon helped create the earth and the world we call home. They're known for their beautiful red eyes, and white and black feathers.

column10: A bee is a flying insect that usually has yellow and black stripes. Bees pollinate plants to help them grow. Worker bees leave the hive, where all of the bees live, and visit different plants to collect little bits called pollen to feed the Queen bee. Every time a bee picks up pollen from a plant, it leaves pollen from another plant, which makes plants able to grow fruit and flowers. There are over 20,000 known types of bees. The Western Honey Bee uses pollen to make honey that you can eat and cook with. People have kept bees to make honey for at least 8,000 years! We know this because there are Spanish cave paintings that show people keeping bees.

A rainbow happens when sunlight shines into tiny drops of water in the air, bounces around inside them, and comes out separated into all its colors: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and violet. Sunlight has all these colors inside all the time; to see them, we need the help of special shapes of clear liquids or solids like water, prisms, or crystals. The water needed to make rainbows has been on Earth for more than 4 billion years–almost as long as Earth has been a planet. For most of our history, humans did not know what made rainbows, so they thought they were magical or mysterious–and they made up many stories about them. About 2000 years ago in Greece, a curious learner named Aristotle described part of the science and reason why we see rainbows in nature. About 700 years ago in Iran, the scientist Kamāl al-Dīn al-Fārisī figured out and explained the real reason rainbows appear. You too might see a rainbow the next time the sun is behind you and rain is falling in front of you.

column10: An orchestra is a large group of people who play different kinds of instruments. There are typically four main sections in an orchestra: the string section, the woodwind section, the brass section, and the percussion section. Orchestras are led by a conductor, who directs the performances using a baton, a wooden rod. The first orchestras came about in the 1600s during the Baroque era. There are many different kinds of orchestras, ranging from school groups to professional groups.

column10:
A unicorn is a fictional creature. It looks like a horse with a long spiral horn on it's forehead, like a narwhal. Unicorns are often drawn with rainbow colored manes and white coats of fur, but they can come in many different colors. Unicorns are said to have various magical powers, and are also said to be fast and hard to catch. Often, when something is extremely rare but very useful, it will be compared to a Unicorn. Many stories include unicorns as characters, from ancient folklore to modern shows and movies.
Magical horses and similar animals have been imagined in various cultures for much of history, but the unicorn as we know came from Ancient Greece. Greek writers like Ctesias record horses with horns and wings living to the east, likely misinterpereting stories about real animals like rhinos and bulls. Later, Unicorns became a key part of folklore in medieval Europe, and even became the national animal of Scotland. Today, unicorns are still popular, with with movies, books, TV shows, and toys featuring them.
